fr:title.text: propositions are closed under retracts

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If B is a retract of A and A is a proposition, then B is a proposition.

fr:title.text: propositions are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0026/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: propositions are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0026/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: This can be seen using the cancellation laws of retractions.

fr:title.text: the truncation hierarchy › closure properties
contractible types are propositions

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If a type A is contractible, then it is a proposition.

fr:title.text: contractible types are propositions › forest://erct/erct-001K/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: As A is contractible with some centre a:A, we have for any x,y:A identifications \chi _x \colon a = x and \chi _y\colon a = y. Therefore, we identify x with y using \chi _x^{-1} \cdot \chi _y.

fr:title.text: propositions are sets

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If a type A is a proposition, then it is a set.

fr:title.text: contractible identification types

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If A is a proposition, then for all x,y:A the identification type x=y is contractible.

fr:title.text: contractible identification types › forest://erct/erct-003X/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The centre of contraction of x=y is given by our assumption that A is a proposition; the contracting identifications are obtained from the same assumption via Lemma .

fr:title.text: n-truncated identification types

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If A is n+1-truncated, then for each x,y:A the identification type x=y is n-truncated.

fr:title.text: n-truncated identification types › forest://erct/erct-003Y/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By induction on n; the base case for n=-2 is given by Corollary , and the other cases are trivial.

fr:title.text: truncation is upward closed

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If a type A is n-truncated, then it is also n+1-truncated.

fr:title.text: truncation is upward closed › forest://erct/erct-002T/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By induction on the truncation level n, with two base cases given by Lemma and Lemma .

fr:title.text: the identity map is contractible

fr:mainmatter.html:p: For any type A, the identity map 1_{A}\colon A\to A is contractible.

fr:title.text: the identity map is contractible › forest://erct/erct-002U/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: the identity map is contractible › forest://erct/erct-002U/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Immediate by identification induction.

fr:title.text: the identity map is n-truncated for all n

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: the identity map is n-truncated for all n

fr:mainmatter.html:p: For any type A, the identity map 1_{A}\colon A\to A is n-truncated for all n.

fr:title.text: the identity map is n-truncated for all n › forest://erct/erct-002V/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: the identity map is n-truncated for all n › forest://erct/erct-002V/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: We proceed by induction on the truncation level n: when n=-2, we recall that the identity map is contractible, and in the inductive step we apply the upward closure of n-truncation to each fiber of 1_{A}.

fr:title.text: propositions are closed under retracts

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If B is a retract of A and A is a proposition, then B is a proposition.

fr:title.text: propositions are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0026/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: propositions are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0026/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: This can be seen using the cancellation laws of retractions.

fr:title.text: contractible types are closed under retracts

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: contractible types are closed under retracts

fr:mainmatter.html:p: If B is a retract of A and A is contractible, then B is a contractible.

fr:title.text: contractible types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0027/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: contractible types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0027/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let s\colon B \to A be a section of r\colon A\to B; if a_0 is the centre of contraction of A, then the centre of contraction of B is given by ra_0. For the rest, we use Lemma and Lemma .

fr:title.text: n-truncated types are closed under retracts

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: n-truncated types are closed under retracts

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Any retract of an n-truncated type is n-truncated.

fr:title.text: n-truncated types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-003S/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: n-truncated types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-003S/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By induction on n; the base cases for n=-2 and n=-1 are given by Corollary and Lemma respectively. In the inductive step, we assume that s\colon B\to A exhibits B as a retract of A and that for each x,y:A the identification type x=_Ay is n+1-truncated to show that for all u,v:B the identification type u=_Bv is likewise n+1-truncated. The result follows from our assumption and the fact that u=_Bv is (by Lemma ) a retract of su=_Asv.

fr:title.text: the coherent remainder of a section is a proposition › forest://erct/erct-0043/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: the coherent remainder of a section is a proposition › forest://erct/erct-0043/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By Lemma , we may argue that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of a proposition. We let P be the following type defined using path object structure of fibers given in Example :
It is not difficult to show using Definition that \mathsf {coherentRemainder}_{f}\sigma is a retract of P; we will not need the further fact that it is equivalent to P.

fr:title.text: being invertible is a proposition › forest://erct/erct-0044/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: being invertible is a proposition › forest://erct/erct-0044/0

fr:title.text: n-truncated types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-003S/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: n-truncated types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-003S/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: By induction on n; the base cases for n=-2 and n=-1 are given by Corollary and Lemma respectively. In the inductive step, we assume that s\colon B\to A exhibits B as a retract of A and that for each x,y:A the identification type x=_Ay is n+1-truncated to show that for all u,v:B the identification type u=_Bv is likewise n+1-truncated. The result follows from our assumption and the fact that u=_Bv is (by Lemma ) a retract of su=_Asv.

fr:title.text: display of right-cloven families of path objects › forest://erct/erct-0034/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: display of right-cloven families of path objects › forest://erct/erct-0034/0

fr:title.text: sections of sections are unique › forest://erct/erct-002Q/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: sections of sections are unique › forest://erct/erct-002Q/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Using the univalence principle for dependent function spaces, we can see that the type of sections of f\colon A\to B is a retract of the fiber of f\circ - at 1_{B}, using the univalence principle for sections.

To show that the type of sections of f is a proposition, we may assume beforehand that we have such a section of f; therefore, because we have assumed a retraction of f already, we know that f is biinvertible and therefore that f is contractible. By Lemma , it suffices to show that the fiber of f\circ - at 1_{B} is a proposition, which follows via Lemma and Lemma .

fr:title.text: contractible types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0027/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: contractible types are closed under retracts › forest://erct/erct-0027/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let s\colon B \to A be a section of r\colon A\to B; if a_0 is the centre of contraction of A, then the centre of contraction of B is given by ra_0. For the rest, we use Lemma and Lemma .

fr:title.text: restriction of path objects along quasi-inverses › forest://erct/erct-001S/0

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: restriction of path objects along quasi-inverses › forest://erct/erct-001S/0

fr:mainmatter.html:p: When s\colon A\to B is a section of r\colon B\to A, we say that A is a retract of B.
A type A is said to be a proposition when every x:A, the type A is contractible with centre x, i.e. all elements of A are identified.
